description: Return something towards a stated earlier or reference condition, feature, access, or arrangement. The reference may be historical, agreed, or chosen for the task; 'talome' does not declare it original, proper, or better merely because it came before.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for unvoiced dental 't' and release into open central 'a'. Touch the tip to the alveolar ridge for stressed 'l' and round into middle-height back 'o'. Close the lips for voiced 'm', let air pass through the nose, and finish with middle-height front 'e'.
sound_symbolism: The dental opening moves through a broad lateral centre and settles at the lips. Its firm but unhurried contour makes a useful companion to work guided by a remembered form.
usage_notes: Its object is the thing moving towards the reference state. Baseline, intended feature, authority, and evidence of success remain available as separate facts. 'shiroka' (repair) restores working function through technical work, while 'phuro' (renew) begins a fresh period of use or validity.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Restoration can concern a wetland, building, practice, or public capacity. Naming the reference state keeps the work accountable instead of letting the hopeful word certify its own success.

semantic_domains.activity: work directed towards a stated earlier or reference condition
semantic_domains.creation: recovery of a feature or arrangement in a made thing
semantic_domains.nature: ecological work towards a selected reference condition
semantic_domains.temporal: a present condition compared with an earlier one
